member.name: T:Etch.Text.Atlas.GlyphAtlas
member.summary: Multi-page glyph atlas. Each page is an independent texture + . Pages manage their own LRU eviction; there is no global LRU layer.

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Atlas.GlyphAtlas.PageCount
member.summary: Number of active pages.

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Atlas.GlyphAtlas.GlyphCount
member.summary: Total glyph count across all pages.

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Atlas.GlyphAtlas.WasExhausted
member.summary: True when, since the last , an insert was refused because the packer was full of other glyphs (recoverable). A glyph refused only for being taller than a shelf does NOT set this — resetting would not make room for it. The presenter checks this between frames and resets, so a churn-exhausted atlas recovers on the next frame instead of silently dropping glyphs forever (WP-3509).

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Atlas.GlyphAtlas.Generation
member.summary: Increments on every . Lets tests observe that a reset actually happened; not otherwise needed for correctness because glyph instances are rebuilt from draw commands every frame.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Atlas.GlyphAtlas.Reset
member.summary: Clears every page's cache and shelf packer (textures retained), bumps , and clears . Must be called between frames — never mid-build — so a frame's atlas uploads stay coherent. Resets all pages together: a partial reset would leave the multi-page lookup inconsistent, and after a reset every visible glyph re-rasterizes into whichever page now has room.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Atlas.GlyphAtlasPage
member.summary: A single page of the multi-page glyph atlas. Wraps one texture + one .

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Atlas.ShelfPack.IsTallerThanShelf(System.Int32)
member.summary: True when a glyph of height can never be placed because, padded, it exceeds the shelf row height. Mirrors the height check in .

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Atlas.LruCache.Reset
member.summary: Clears every cached glyph and resets the shelf packer to empty. The backing texture is left untouched — its stale pixels are overwritten as glyphs re-rasterize, and the 1px shelf padding prevents any sampling bleed in the meantime. Used for the generation reset (WP-3509) when the packer exhausts: cheap, and safe because glyph instances are rebuilt from draw commands every frame, so no UV outlives the reset.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Atlas.LruCache.IsTallerThanShelf(System.Int32)
member.summary: True when a glyph of this height can never fit a shelf (taller than the packer's row height, padding included), so a would not help. Distinguishes recoverable packer exhaustion from a glyph that is fundamentally too large for the atlas geometry.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.FontHinting
member.summary: Controls how aggressively a font is hinted during rasterization. This affects the trade-off between sharpness (snapping to pixel grid) and fidelity (preserving designed glyph shapes).

member.name: F:Etch.Text.FontHinting.None
member.summary: No hinting. Glyphs are rendered exactly as designed, which produces the most faithful shapes but can look blurry at small sizes. Equivalent to FT_LOAD_NO_HINTING.

member.name: F:Etch.Text.FontHinting.Slight
member.summary: Light hinting (Apple-style). Only subtle vertical snapping to improve consistency without distorting glyph shapes. Preferred for high-DPI displays and small text where full hinting looks too aggressive. Equivalent to FT_LOAD_TARGET_LIGHT.

member.name: F:Etch.Text.FontHinting.Normal
member.summary: Normal hinting (default). FreeType's autohinter snaps edges to the pixel grid for sharper text at the cost of some shape distortion. Equivalent to FT_LOAD_DEFAULT.

member.name: F:Etch.Text.FontHinting.Full
member.summary: Full hinting (Windows-style). Uses the font's embedded bytecode instructions for maximum grid-fitting. Can look very sharp but may significantly distort shapes, especially at larger sizes. Equivalent to FT_LOAD_TARGET_NORMAL with bytecode interpreter.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Outline.GlyphOutlineBuilder.IsCompositeGlyph(Etch.Text.Shape.FontFace,System.UInt16)
member.summary: Returns true if the glyph is a composite glyph (uses other glyphs as components).

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Outline.GlyphOutlineBuilder.TryGetCachedPalette(Etch.Text.Shape.FontFace,Etch.Text.Outline.GlyphColorValue[]@)
member.summary: Parses the CPAL table once per font face and caches the result. Returns false if the font has no CPAL table or the palette is malformed.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Outline.GlyphOutlineBuilder.HasColorLayers(Etch.Text.Shape.FontFace,System.UInt16)
member.summary: Returns true if the font has a COLR table and the glyph has color layers.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Outline.GlyphOutlineBuilder.TryGetColorLayers(Etch.Text.Shape.FontFace,System.UInt16,Etch.Text.Outline.ColorLayer[]@,Etch.Text.Outline.GlyphColorValue[]@)
member.summary: Parses the COLR table for the glyph and returns the cached CPAL palette. CPAL is parsed once per font face and reused; COLR is parsed per-glyph.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Outline.ColorLayer
member.summary: A single layer in a COLR color glyph: a glyph ID and a palette index.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Outline.ColorLayer.#ctor(System.UInt16,System.UInt16)
member.summary: A single layer in a COLR color glyph: a glyph ID and a palette index.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Outline.GlyphColorValue
member.summary: Simple RGBA color value for CPAL palette entries.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Outline.GlyphColorValue.#ctor(System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Simple RGBA color value for CPAL palette entries.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.SubpixelQuant
member.summary: Quantizes a fractional pixel X offset to a small finite set of subpixel positions. This bounds the glyph-cache key space while preserving smooth horizontal spacing.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.QuantizedSubpixel.QuantizeX(System.Single,Etch.Text.SubpixelQuant)
member.summary: Returns the quantized subpixel bucket for . For the result is 0..3; for it is 0..7. Negative values wrap correctly (e.g. –0.3 → same bucket as 0.7).

member.name: M:Etch.Text.QuantizedSubpixel.Dequantize(System.Byte,Etch.Text.SubpixelQuant)
member.summary: Converts a quantized bucket back to a pixel offset.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Rasterize.GlyphRasterizer.RasterizeColorGlyph(Etch.Text.Shape.FontFace,System.UInt16,System.Span{System.Byte},System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Renders a COLR color glyph to an RGBA buffer. Returns true if the glyph was successfully rendered as a color glyph. Uses FreeType for measurement and rendering when available to ensure correct results for composite glyphs and consistent coordinate systems.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Shape.FontFace.GetSubpixelVariant
member.summary: Returns a cached FontFace with 3× horizontal scale for subpixel rendering. Shares the immutable Face/Blob. Do not dispose the returned instance — it is owned by this FontFace and disposed along with it.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Shape.ShapingCache
member.summary: LRU cache for shaped text runs. Keyed by the full shaping signature (text hash, face id, size, BiDi level, script tag). On cache hit the original text is re-compared to guard against hash collisions.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Shape.ShapingCache.TryShape(Etch.Text.Shape.ShapeRequest,Etch.Text.Shape.ShapedRun@)
member.summary: Attempt to retrieve a cached for . On miss, shapes via and inserts the result.

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Shape.ShapingCache.Count
member.summary: Current number of cached entries.

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Shape.ShapingCache.Capacity
member.summary: Maximum number of entries before LRU eviction.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Shape.ShapingKey
member.summary: Immutable key for the shaping cache.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.SubpixelOrientation
member.summary: LCD subpixel orientation for a text rendering surface.

member.name: F:Etch.Text.SubpixelOrientation.Grayscale
member.summary: Standard grayscale anti-aliasing (default).

member.name: F:Etch.Text.SubpixelOrientation.RgbHorizontal
member.summary: Horizontal RGB stripe order (standard Windows LCD).

member.name: T:Etch.Text.SurfaceInfo
member.summary: Surface configuration for text rendering.

member.name: F:Etch.Text.SurfaceInfo.SubpixelOrientation
member.summary: Subpixel orientation for this surface. Defaults to .

member.name: M:Etch.Text.SurfaceInfo.ShouldUseSubpixel(Etch.Geometry.Affine)
member.summary: Returns true if the given transform is axis-aligned (no rotation or skew) and the surface is configured for subpixel rendering.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiAlgorithm
member.summary: Minimal but correct implementation of the Unicode Bidirectional Algorithm (UAX #9). Covers Latin, Arabic, and Hebrew. Uses stackalloc for paragraphs ≤ 256 chars; ArrayPool fallback above.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiAlgorithm.Analyze(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.SByte)
member.summary: Analyse a single paragraph and return visual-order runs.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiAlgorithm.ResolveBracketPairs(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Span{Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.SByte})
member.summary: N0: resolve the direction of paired brackets so that e.g. parentheses in an opposite-direction context adopt the surrounding direction. BD16 pairs brackets with a bounded stack; N0 then sets each pair from the strong types it encloses (numbers counting as R) and the preceding context.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass
member.summary: Unicode Bidi_Class property values per UAX #9. Only values needed for the core algorithm are included.

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.L
member.summary: Left-to-Right — strong LTR (Latin, CJK, etc.)

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.R
member.summary: Right-to-Left — strong RTL (Hebrew, etc.)

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.AL
member.summary: Arabic Letter — strong RTL (Arabic, Syriac, etc.)

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.EN
member.summary: European Number — weak EN

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.ES
member.summary: European Number Separator — weak ES

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.ET
member.summary: European Number Terminator — weak ET

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.AN
member.summary: Arabic Number — weak AN

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.CS
member.summary: Common Number Separator — weak CS

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.NSM
member.summary: Nonspacing Mark — weak NSM

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.BN
member.summary: Boundary Neutral — weak BN

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.B
member.summary: Paragraph Separator — neutral B

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.S
member.summary: Segment Separator — neutral S

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.WS
member.summary: Whitespace — neutral WS

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.ON
member.summary: Other Neutral — neutral ON

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.LRE
member.summary: Left-to-Right Embedding — explicit LRE

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.LRO
member.summary: Left-to-Right Override — explicit LRO

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.RLE
member.summary: Right-to-Left Embedding — explicit RLE

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.RLO
member.summary: Right-to-Left Override — explicit RLO

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.PDF
member.summary: Pop Directional Format — explicit PDF

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.LRI
member.summary: Left-to-Right Isolate — explicit LRI

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.RLI
member.summary: Right-to-Left Isolate — explicit RLI

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.FSI
member.summary: First Strong Isolate — explicit FSI

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClass.PDI
member.summary: Pop Directional Isolate — explicit PDI

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClassTable
member.summary: Lookup table for Bidi_Class. Covers ASCII, Hebrew, Arabic, and common punctuation. Unknown code points default to ON (safe neutral fallback).

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiClassTable.Get(System.Char)
member.summary: Return the Bidi_Class for a Unicode code point.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiRunDirection
member.summary: The resolved direction of a BiDi run.

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiRunDirection.Ltr
member.summary: Left-to-right (even embedding level).

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiRunDirection.Rtl
member.summary: Right-to-left (odd embedding level).

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiRun
member.summary: A single run of text with uniform direction.

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiRun.Start
member.summary: Start index in the original string (inclusive).

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiRun.Length
member.summary: Length in characters.

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiRun.Level
member.summary: Embedding level (even = LTR, odd = RTL).

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiRun.Direction
member.summary: Resolved direction derived from level parity.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiParagraphResult
member.summary: Result of BiDi analysis on a paragraph.

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiParagraphResult.ParagraphLevel
member.summary: Resolved paragraph embedding level (0 = LTR, 1 = RTL).

member.name: P:Etch.Text.Unicode.Minimal.BidiParagraphResult.Runs
member.summary: Visual-order runs, left-to-right in display order.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Unicode.LineBreakKind
member.summary: The kind of line break returned by .

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.LineBreakKind.Soft
member.summary: A soft break — text may wrap here.

member.name: F:Etch.Text.Unicode.LineBreakKind.Hard
member.summary: A hard break — forced line ending (\n, \r\n, LS, PS).

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Unicode.LineBreakIterator
member.summary: Streaming, zero-allocation-per-iteration line-break enumerator. Wraps ICU4N with per-locale, thread-local instance caching.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Unicode.LineBreakIterator.#ctor(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.String)
member.summary: Create an iterator over .
member.param: Text to scan for line-break opportunities.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Unicode.LineBreakIterator.MoveNext(System.Int32@,Etch.Text.Unicode.LineBreakKind@)
member.summary: Advance to the next break boundary.
member.returns: true if a boundary was found; false when exhausted.

member.name: T:Etch.Text.Unicode.UnicodeData
member.summary: Process-global ICU4N initialisation and health checks. Call once before using any ICU4N-dependent API (line-breaking, BiDi, etc.).

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Unicode.UnicodeData.Ensure
member.summary: Verifies that ICU4N data is loaded and functional. Panics with if not. Safe to call from multiple threads; idempotent.

member.name: M:Etch.Text.Unicode.UnicodeData.GetParagraphRuns(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.SByte)
member.summary: Analyse a single paragraph and return visual-order BiDi runs. Uses our own UAX #9 implementation because ICU4N does not expose a public BiDi class.
